frameworks.name: mesoscale functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: interregional functional network reorganization tracked across learning at single-unit resolution

frameworks.name: distributed coding
frameworks.one_line: task-relevant information is distributed across multiple cortical and subcortical regions

core_question: How do regional nodes in a distributed brain network reorganize their functional interactions throughout decision-making learning?
key_assumptions: interregional functional connectivity can be estimated from chronic large-scale single-unit recordings
network rank meaningfully orders regions in a learning-dependent hierarchy
manipulations: learning stage (across acquisition of a visual decision-making task)
trial type (correct No-Go vs other responses)
key_findings: learning reshaped interregional functional connectivity across 10 cortical and subcortical regions
a subnetwork involving visual and frontal regions emerged during acquisition of correct No-Go responses
visual stimulus representation became more widespread across regions with learning
a region's network rank predicted the peak timing of its visual information encoding
interpretations: mesoscale networks undergo dynamic restructuring during learning
functional connectivity rank influences the propagation of sensory information across the network
